task_completion.description: Configure the Spacecraft state and epoch in GMAT based on the provided memo.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing the Spacecraft properties window with the Epoch set to '01 Jan 2026 00:00:00.000' and orbital elements populated.

task_completion.description: Configure a high-fidelity force model including Earth's oblateness and Sun/Moon point masses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT Force Model configuration window showing Earth Gravity Degree and Order set to 4 or higher, and Point Masses explicitly including 'Sun' and 'Luna' (Moon).

task_completion.description: Set up GMAT to calculate or report the solar beta angle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing a ReportFile, XYPlot, or Variable configuration where 'Spacecraft.Earth.BetaAngle' is selected for output or calculation.

task_completion.description: Set the mission sequence to propagate for 180 days.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT Mission Sequence or Propagate command window showing the stopping condition set to 180 days (e.g., 'Spacecraft.ElapsedDays = 180' or similar).
GMAT GUI showing the simulation running or completed, evidenced by a generated plot of the beta angle, populated report file, or the message window indicating a successful run.

task_completion.description: Record the maximum absolute beta angle and total days above 60 degrees in the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of a file in ~/GMAT_output (e.g., iss_beta_report.txt). The file must state a maximum absolute beta angle between 68.0 and 76.0, and a high beta days count between 10 and 25.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used GMAT to perform the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with the GMAT GUI or GMAT command-line execution, not just a Python script or text editor bypassing the required tool.

integrity.description: Verify the recorded findings match the simulation output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final recorded max beta angle and days > 60 in the text report should logically derive from the GMAT plots or reports shown in earlier screenshots, not hallucinated.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just create the final report with guessed values.
integrity.visual_evidence: There must be evidence of the simulation running and producing data before the final report file is created and saved.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial state memo is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/iss_state_memo.txt'. The expected output report path is '/home/ga/GMAT_output/iss_beta_report.txt'. A correct 180-day GMAT simulation will yield a maximum absolute beta angle between 68.0 and 76.0 degrees (true value ~71.5), and the total number of days spent above 60.0 degrees will be between 10 and 25 days (true value ~14).
